user_query:
I need you to normalize a messy OpenClaw setup into the Fire Dragon Fruit Architecture. Use the config in input/system.json as the single source of truth. Do not assume today’s date; only use the date provided in input/system.json. Deliver a clean main workspace and a minimal, isolated rescue workspace.

Read: input/system.json (project name, goal, criteria, tasks, daily facts, and scheduling windows) and input/legacy_readme.md (background only; do not inject it into the always-on root workspace files).

Create the following outputs exactly under the specified paths:

1) Main workspace (under output/workspace/):
- output/workspace/MEMORY.md
  - Must include the exact phrase "Architecture: Fire Dragon Fruit".
  - Must mention there is one strong "main" and one isolated "rescue".
  - Must state that active projects are listed in projects/INDEX.md.
- output/workspace/AGENTS.md
  - Must describe one primary main and an isolated rescue (no lab), and state that their memories must not be mixed.
- output/workspace/HEARTBEAT.md
  - Must contain the exact string "HEARTBEAT_OK" (as the no-change output) and a short checklist referencing real files.
- output/workspace/AUTOMATION.md
  - Must explicitly include the heartbeat working window from input/system.json (e.g., 09:00-22:00) and the reflect time (e.g., 23:30).
  - Must reference both of these real files by relative path: projects/<project>/PROGRESS.md and memory/<daily_log_date>.md (using the date from input/system.json).
- output/workspace/memory/<daily_log_date>.md
  - Must include all today_facts items from input/system.json, each as a separate line.
- output/workspace/memory/topics/user-prefs.md (can be stubbed but must exist)
- output/workspace/memory/topics/workflows.md
  - Must include the exact line: "Promotion path: raw facts -> daily logs -> topic files -> MEMORY.md".
- output/workspace/projects/INDEX.md
  - Must list the active project by its name from input/system.json and point to projects/<project>/PRD.md.
- output/workspace/projects/<project>/PRD.md
  - Must include the project goal and all success criteria from input/system.json.
- output/workspace/projects/<project>/EXECUTION_PLAN.md
  - Must enumerate all execution_tasks from input/system.json.
- output/workspace/projects/<project>/PROGRESS.md
  - Must include the risk line from input/system.json notes (e.g., "Risk: vendor API rate limits during peak hours").
- output/workspace/skills/reflect-mode/SKILL.md
  - Must include a heading or line with "Reflect Mode" and explicitly state that it reads memory/YYYY-MM-DD.md and projects/*/PROGRESS.md.
- output/workspace/skills/scout-mode/SKILL.md (must exist and include a heading with "Scout Mode").
- output/workspace/skills/closer-mode/SKILL.md (must exist and include a heading with "Closer Mode").
- output/workspace/skills/ops-mode/SKILL.md (must exist and include a heading with "Ops Mode").

2) Rescue workspace (under output/rescue/):
- output/rescue/AGENTS.md
  - Must clearly say it is an emergency continuity agent and "not a second main".
- output/rescue/MEMORY.md (minimal, can be stubbed but must exist)

Rules:
- Do not create a lab agent. Keep it to one main and one rescue.
- Keep rescue isolated; do not reference or depend on main’s daily memory in rescue docs.
- All scheduled tasks must reference real files by relative paths under output/workspace/.
- Do not place background research or arbitrary .md content in the root of output/workspace/ beyond the files explicitly requested above.

Write all outputs only under output/ as specified. Make no other files. Confirm you used the project name, goals, success criteria, tasks, dates, today_facts, and schedule windows from input/system.json exactly in the appropriate files.
